text:
<think>I should begin by clarifying what PISA is and the context of the scores to ensure the reader understands the basis for comparison.</think>
PISA, the Programme for International Student Assessment, evaluates educational systems worldwide by testing the skills and knowledge of 15-year-old students in reading, mathematics, and science every three years.
<think>Now, I should specify which countries are considered part of Northern Europe for the purposes of this question, to avoid ambiguity.</think>
Northern Europe typically includes countries such as Denmark, Estonia, Finland, Iceland, Latvia, Lithuania, Norway, and Sweden.
<think>I should now address which country among these has performed best on PISA since 2015, focusing on their average scores and international rankings.</think>
Since 2015, Estonia has consistently achieved the highest PISA scores among Northern European countries, performing exceptionally well in all three tested subjects.
<think>It would be useful to mention how Estonia's scores compare internationally for added context.</think>
Estonia has ranked among the top countries globally, especially in science and reading, often surpassing not only its regional neighbors but also most OECD countries.
<think>I should conclude by summarizing why Estonia is considered the top performer in Northern Europe based on PISA results.</think>
Therefore, Estonia is widely recognized as having the best PISA scores in Northern Europe since 2015.
